target.name: Lightmatter
target.founder: Nicholas Harris, Darius Bunandar, Thomas Graham

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $420M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Lightmatter is a Series C semiconductor chip design company that has raised $420M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 6 private peers in the same sector and stage, including d-Matrix ($2.0B Series C), Tenstorrent ($2.7B Series D), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B Series D), Etched ($5.0B Series B), and Positron AI ($1.0B Series B). Three public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) exist but cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $420M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yielding $714M. Peer-median valuation across the six comparables produces $2.4B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. The dispersion reflects stage variation within the peer set, as adjacent-stage companies (Series B and Series D) command valuations from $1.0B to $5.0B.

These methods support a triangulated valuation range of $607M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.5B. The wide spread reflects uncertainty around Lightmatter's relative competitive position, commercial traction, and technical differentiation within a crowded frontier-chip segment. Stage-progression anchors a conservative view; peer-median reflects market pricing of comparable risk profiles.
